LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SPECTRUM SETTLEMENT RECOVERY LLC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. CALIFORNIA HAS "A STRONG POLICY IN FAVOR OF LIBERAL ALLOWANCE OF AMENDMENTS." (MESLER V. BRAGG MGMT. (1985) 39 CAL.3D 290, 296.) AS DEFENDANTS CONCEDE, THIS APPLIES TO VERIFIED PLEADINGS. (OPP. 1:10.) PLAINTIFF ADEQUATELY EXPLAINS WHY THE AMENDMENT IS NECESSARY. (SEE, E.G., MORGANSTEIN DEC.) COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR# 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-13-2019 Set For Trial On Feb-24-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar May-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF SPECTRUM SETTLEMENT RECOVERY LLC's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint.

Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is GRANTED. California has "a strong policy in favor of liberal allowance of amendments." (Mesler v. Bragg Mgmt. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 290, 296.) As defendants concede, this applies to verified pleadings. (Opp. 1:10.) Plaintiff adequately explains why the amendment is necessary. (See, e.g., Morganstein Dec.)
Counsel for plaintiff is to prepare a proposed and submit proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
